title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Colquhoun questions Mrs. Lucas regarding the work habits of Mrs. Hemmings. Mrs. Lucas complains that the widow is often absent during work hours and suggests she send her children to summer camps, a suggestion Mrs. Hemmings reportedly rejected. Colquhoun notes that Mrs. Hemmings's wages are lower than most.

In a separate interrogation, Mrs. Sweetnam describes the robbery to Edmund, recounting how the lights went out and a figure appeared with a revolver, demanding "Money or your life!" She describes a bullet whizzing past them and a flashlight being shone in their eyes. Mrs. Sweetnam recalls being near the clock and fireplace. Edmund states he was in the middle of the long room with Julia Simmons. He notes that Philipa Hemmings was by the fireplace on the far side of the room. Edmund also clarifies that the dining room door was locked from the outside.

Detective Kolar listens to Colonel Eastbrook, who provides a psychological theory for the crime. The Colonel argues that the perpetrator placed the advertisement to gain attention and intended to play the role of a "tough guy," but lost control when the situation became real. He suggests the man fired aimlessly, panicked after hitting someone, and then committed suicide. The Colonel and his wife claim they were standing by a table with flowers during the shooting.

Finally, Inspector Corradino questions Miss Chickering in a pig pen. She states she was by the fireplace during the incident and expresses confusion as to why the man did not rob someone on the road instead. She recalls the flashlight blinding the group and mentions that she initially thought Patrick Simmons might have been playing with a loaded revolver.
